notes: Paper 1.5 C4 baseline (N=4 reps, CV=8.0%). Scalar Phase-1 unpack + SIMD Phase-2 dot. Node.js v24 + wasm SIMD strict.

Paper 1.5 C4 vectorized Phase-1 unpack using wasm_v128_or/and/shr/shl. N=4 reps, CV=2.5%. Bit-exact match (result=56754044928 on all 8 runs). Speedup modest — LLVM -O3 auto-vectorizes most of the scalar loop already.

E2E Qwen 2.5 0.5B Q6_K (620 MB GGUF, sha256 2f8223…b97c7f5) on local dfx canister, baseline (scalar Q6_K unpack) build. N_MAX = 25 tok/call, 3/3 reps deterministic, identical decode 'The capital of France is Paris.' Boundary verified: N=26 fails 3/3 with IC0522.

E2E same model + canister setup, patched (vectorized) build. N_MAX = 25 tok/call — same as baseline. The +2.3% microbench gain is hidden by integer-token granularity at this scale (2.3% × 25 = 0.575 tokens, < 1). Decode bit-identical to baseline (same 25-token completion ending in 'The correct answer'). E2E speedup invisible on this metric here, but kernel-level improvement, bit-exactness, and 3.79× CV reduction (microbench) remain the C4 contribution.
